title: Financial Literacy for Professionals
description: Take control of your money — budgeting, saving, investing, and planning for the future — at any income level.

modules.title: Budgeting and Cash Flow
modules.description: Know where your money goes and design a plan that actually works.

lessons.title: The 50/30/20 Framework
lessons.content:
# The 50/30/20 Budgeting Framework

A simple, flexible starting point for managing your income.

## The Split

- **50 % — Needs** — rent/mortgage, utilities, groceries, transport, insurance, minimum debt payments.
- **30 % — Wants** — dining out, entertainment, subscriptions, hobbies, travel.
- **20 % — Savings & Investments** — emergency fund, pension contributions, investments, extra debt repayments.

## How to Apply It
1. Calculate your monthly after-tax income.
2. Multiply by 0.5, 0.3, and 0.2 to get your target amounts.
3. Track spending for one month against these targets.
4. Adjust — life does not fit perfectly into percentages.

## When to Adjust
- High cost-of-living area: needs may exceed 50 %.
- Aggressively paying off debt: reduce wants to 15–20 % temporarily.
- High earner: increase savings rate beyond 20 %.

modules.title: Saving and Investing Fundamentals
modules.description: Put your money to work for the long term.

lessons.title: The Power of Compound Growth
lessons.content:
# The Power of Compound Growth

Einstein reportedly called compound interest the eighth wonder of the world. Whether or not that is true, the maths is undeniable.

## How Compounding Works

When your investment earns a return, that return is added to your principal. Next period, you earn a return on the larger amount.

**Example:**
- Invest £5,000 at 7 % per year.
- After 10 years: ~£9,836 (without adding anything).
- After 30 years: ~£38,061.

## The Time Variable
Time is the most powerful factor. Starting at 25 vs. 35 can double your retirement pot — even contributing the same monthly amount.

## The Three Levers
1. **How much you invest** — even small amounts matter.
2. **How long you invest** — start as early as possible.
3. **The return rate** — diversified index funds historically average 7–10 % per year.

## Key Principle
You do not need to be a stock picker. Low-cost index funds + time = the most reliable path to wealth for most people.

lessons.title: ISAs, Pensions and Tax-Efficient Investing
lessons.content:
# ISAs, Pensions and Tax-Efficient Investing

Growth inside tax-advantaged accounts is significantly faster because you keep more of your returns.

## Pensions (UK)
- Contributions are tax-relieved — the government tops up your contributions at your marginal rate.
- Employer matching is free money — always contribute at least enough to get the full match.
- Money grows tax-free inside the pension wrapper.

## ISAs (UK)
- Up to £20,000 per year can be invested with zero tax on growth or withdrawals.
- **Cash ISA** — savings, FSCS-protected, lower returns.
- **Stocks & Shares ISA** — invest in funds, shares, ETFs.
- **Lifetime ISA** — 25 % government bonus on up to £4,000/year for first home or retirement.

## Recommended Order of Priority
1. Employer pension match (free money).
2. Emergency fund (3–6 months expenses).
3. High-interest debt repayment.
4. ISA allowance.
5. Additional pension contributions.
